title: Rhino Build-Plan IR
description: Validated intermediate representation (IR) shared by the text-to-model, image-to-model and rhino-scene-state skills. It externalizes the full spatial intent of a model so the LLM can re-read world state each step instead of holding it in context. Every numeric value is expressed in the document 'units' against the named 'world_frame'. Producers (text-to-model, image-to-model) write 'object'..'verify'; rhino-scene-state appends realized GUIDs out-of-band in the scene-graph artifact, not here. The build pipeline reads this IR to emit guarded geometry codegen and to drive the render+measure+repair loop.

object.description: Plain-language name of the thing being modeled, e.g. 'four-legged dining chair'. Used in verify binary_questions and in part provenance.

units.description: Document unit system. Must be set on sc.doc BEFORE any geometry is created. Every dims/origin/penetration/height number in this IR is in these units.

tolerance.description: Absolute model tolerance in 'units'. At runtime always prefer the live sc.doc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance; this value is the IR's intended tolerance for join/offset/boolean operations and for naked-edge checks.

world_frame.description: Named base construction plane that all part frames are relative to. Default WorldXY. Parts relocate from Plane.WorldXY to their target plane via Transform.PlaneToPlane.

scale.description: How absolute size was determined. Text pipeline typically value_source='stated' with high confidence; image pipeline typically 'reference_object' or 'metrology_assumption' with a range + medium/low confidence, which forces the verify loop onto scale-invariant ratio_checks only (correction C5).

value_source.description: stated = a real dimension was given in the prompt (verify absolutes). reference_object = scaled off a known object in an image (carry confidence). metrology_assumption = a category default was assumed (lowest trust).

overall_height_mm.description: Overall bounding height in millimetres. A single number when known, or a [min,max] inclusive range when scale is uncertain (image pipeline). Always mm regardless of document 'units'.

oneOf.description: [min, max] inclusive range; max must be >= min (enforced in producer).

confidence.description: Confidence in the absolute scale. low/medium => verify loop fires repairs on ratio_checks only, never on absolute numeric_checks.

provenance.description: Where the scale came from, e.g. 'prompt: 450mm seat height' or 'reference: A4 sheet 297mm in image'.

assumption.description: The explicit assumption made when value_source='metrology_assumption', e.g. 'standard dining chair seat height ~450mm'.

symmetry.description: Declared symmetries of the object. Used both to reduce part authoring (mirror/array) and to generate symmetry verify questions.

plane.description: Mirror plane, e.g. 'WorldYZ' or a named frame. Required when type='mirror'.

axis.description: Rotational axis, e.g. 'WorldZ'. Required when type='rotational'.

origin.description: Origin point of the symmetry plane/axis in 'units' against world_frame.

count.description: Number of instances around a rotational axis (optional, rotational only).

params.description: Named scalar parameters that parts/relations reference symbolically (e.g. 'seat_height', 'leg_radius'). Keeps proportions consistent and makes ratio_checks computable. All values are plain numbers in 'units'.

stages.description: Ordered build STAGES for scoped, idempotent, checkpointed emit. Each stage names a slice of 'parts' (by part id) that is baked + reconciled as one unit, then checkpointed, before the next stage runs. A part with no stage belongs to an implicit 'default' stage. Stages exist so the double-execution wrapper self-corrects WITHIN a stage (the stage deletes its own objects by tag, then re-creates them once) and so a fragile-op failure or a later edit rebuilds only its stage, never the whole model (see conventions §12).

id.description: Stable stage id. Stamped as UserString 'stage' on every object baked in this stage and used as the scoped delete key for idempotent re-emit (conventions §12).

parts.description: Ordered part ids built in this stage (a subset of the top-level 'parts'). The same id must not appear in two stages.

depends_on.description: Stage ids that must be baked + checkpointed before this stage (e.g. a 'bell_chamber' stage depends_on 'shaft'). Used to order emit and to know which downstream stages to re-verify after a re-emit.

parts.description: Ordered list of parts. Each part is EITHER a primitive (has 'primitive') OR an operation (has 'operation'), never both. Operations may consume earlier parts by id via their input fields.

boolean_plan.description: Explicit, ordered boolean execution plan. Each step names an op, the input part ids, and the id of the resulting part. Mirrors any boolean operation parts but gives the executor a single linear sequence to run and to volume-check (correction C2).

inputs.description: Part ids consumed by this boolean. For difference, inputs[0] is the base; the rest are subtracted.

result.description: Part id of the boolean result.

verify.description: Acceptance criteria evaluated by render-and-look + analyze_objects. binary_questions and compare_to_reference go to vision (profile fidelity / 'looks like X'); numeric_checks and ratio_checks go to bbox/analyze math (vision-demotion, correction C4).

binary_questions.description: Yes/no questions answered by colored-part vision, e.g. 'is the red seat above the blue legs?'. Phrase so the answer depends on relative position/color, not measured size.

numeric_checks.description: Absolute measurements (only trustworthy when scale.confidence=high, i.e. text pipeline).

ratio_checks.description: Scale-invariant ratios (e.g. seat_height/overall_height). The ONLY checks the image pipeline may fire repairs on (correction C5).

compare_to_reference.description: When true, render-and-look diffs the rendered model against the reference image/silhouette (image pipeline).

point3d.description: [x, y, z] in document 'units' against world_frame.

frame.description: Explicit construction plane a part is built on. The part is authored on Plane.WorldXY then moved to this frame via Transform.PlaneToPlane.

plane.description: Named target plane, e.g. 'WorldXY', 'WorldYZ', 'WorldXZ', or a custom frame name resolved by the executor. Default WorldXY when omitted.

x_axis.description: Optional explicit plane X axis vector.

y_axis.description: Optional explicit plane Y axis vector.

expect.description: Expected value: a number for numeric_checks, or a number/ratio for ratio_checks.

tol.description: Acceptance tolerance for |measured - expect|, in 'units' for numeric_checks or absolute ratio delta for ratio_checks.

axis_line.description: An axis expressed as two points (revolve axis, etc). Must be coplanar with the revolve profile (correction C7).

line.description: [[x0,y0,z0],[x1,y1,z1]] start and end of the axis.

relation.description: A spatial/logical relation between this part and another. 'penetration' is REQUIRED when type='interpenetrate' (parts feeding a boolean union must overlap 0.5-2mm, correction C3).

this.description: Part id this relation is anchored on (defaults to the owning part).

to.description: The other part id (or symmetry/frame name for symmetric_about).

penetration.description: Overlap depth in 'units' (typically 0.5-2mm) for interpenetrate relations feeding a boolean union. Coincident/coplanar contact is degenerate and forbidden for unions.

part.description: A primitive part (has 'primitive') or an operation part (has 'operation'). Exactly one of those two keys must be present.

id.description: Stable part identifier. Becomes the UserString 'part_id' on the baked object and the scene-graph key mapping to its GUID (correction C1).

stage.description: Build stage this part belongs to (conventions §12). Stamped as UserString 'stage' at bake; the scoped idempotent delete key. If the top-level 'stages' array is present it is authoritative; this inline field is a shorthand for plans that don't need explicit stage ordering. A part with neither belongs to the implicit 'default' stage.

provenance.description: Why this part exists / where its shape came from (prompt phrase, reference silhouette, archetype profile).

primitive.description: Primitive type. 'interpolated_curve' builds a Curve.CreateInterpolatedCurve through control_points (used as loft sections / sweep rails / revolve generatrices).

frame.description: Construction frame for a primitive part.

dims.description: Primitive dimensions in 'units', e.g. box {x,y,z}, cylinder {radius,height}, sphere {radius}, cone {radius,height}, plane {x,y}. Keys depend on 'primitive'.

control_points.description: Ordered control/interpolation points for 'interpolated_curve' (and reusable as section/rail/generatrix geometry).

count.description: Number of instances of this part (e.g. 4 legs).

instances.description: Explicit per-instance frames when 'count' instances are not a simple array.

instances_at.description: Explicit instance origin points (shorthand for instances with default plane).

operation.description: Operation type. Each requires its own fields below.

generatrix.description: Part id of the profile curve to revolve (revolve). Must touch the revolve axis at start AND end, or be closed (correction C6).

axis.description: Revolve axis as a two-point line, coplanar with the generatrix (revolve).

angle_deg.description: Revolve sweep angle in degrees (revolve; 360 for full).

sections.description: Ordered part ids of section curves to loft through (loft). All must run the same direction and be seam-aligned (correction C7).

rail.description: Part id of the rail curve (sweep1). Must be G1 continuous (correction C7).

thickness.description: Shell wall thickness in 'units' (shell). Must be < min feature size; brep must be closed/capped before shelling (correction C6).

open_face.description: Face index, or list of face indices, left open by the shell (shell).

cap.description: Whether to cap the result into a closed solid (revolve/extrude/sweep1). Required before shell.

inputs.description: Part ids consumed by this operation (boolean/loft/extrude inputs). For boolean difference, inputs[0] is the base.

result.description: Part id of the operation result (defaults to this part's own id).

relations.description: Relations this part has to others. Union inputs must declare an 'interpenetrate' relation with a penetration depth (correction C3).
